summary.en: Sell less of more. A platform aggregates revenue across thousands of niche products that each sell rarely, sustained by low inventory cost, strong search/recommendation, and democratized production and distribution.
summary.zh: 卖更多种类、每种卖更少。平台把成千上万只偶尔卖出的小众商品汇聚起来,合计销量足以匹敌少数爆款 —— 关键依赖低库存成本、强搜索/推荐,以及生产和分发渠道的民主化。

sources.label: Chris Anderson · The Long Tail · Hyperion · 2006
Chris Anderson · 'The Long Tail' · Wired · October 2004
Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley · 2010 (pp. 66-75)

note.en: Originating Wired article. Coined the term and the curve diagram. Identified Amazon, Netflix, and Rhapsody as exemplars of niche-aggregated revenue.
note.zh: 首次提出长尾概念的 Wired 文章。命名了「长尾」一词与那条特征曲线,把 Amazon、Netflix、Rhapsody 列为代表案例。

references.label: Chris Anderson · The Long Tail: Why the Future of Business Is Selling Less of More · Hyperion

note.en: Book-length expansion. Defines the three economic forces (democratized production, democratized distribution, falling search costs) and the curation requirement that separates a real long tail from noise.
note.zh: 书籍版的完整扩展。定义了三大经济驱动因素(生产工具民主化、分发渠道民主化、搜索成本下降),以及把「真长尾」和「噪音」区分开的内容筛选要求。

references.label: Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley

note.en: Adapts the long tail into the BMC pattern catalog (Pattern No. 2). Featured paradigm cases: LEGO (Factory + Ideas) and Lulu.com — both shipped in this library.
note.zh: 把长尾收录为 BMC 模式手册(第 2 号模式)。书中重点案例:LEGO(Factory + Ideas)与 Lulu.com —— 本案例库均已收录。
